claim: Maoist political language structured individual life and death as meaningful only through service to 'the people' as an eternal collective body, inverting private survival as the supreme value and demanding selfless self-abnegation — including willingness to die — as the highest moral obligation.

confidence.label: moderate-high
confidence.rationale: Both passages are from Mao's Selected Works (public domain, verified via Marxists.org). 'Serve the People' explicitly codes death-for-the-people as weightier than personal survival. 'In Memory of Bethune' codes absolute selflessness as the highest virtue. Together they establish the collective-immortality-to-sacrifice mechanism at the level of the symbolic order's core texts. Confidence capped pending broader periodization review.
